laws.title: Public Law 3-24 | Judgeship Reducation Act of 2022
laws.status_basis: The source text is an operative structural overhaul: it changes Supreme Court seats, closes the Court of Appeals, makes the D.C. district court the only federal district court, sets magistrate count, and expressly repeals prior acts. No later enactment in the reviewed materials fully repeals it.

provisions.text_summary: Reduces the Supreme Court to 1 Chief Justice and 4 Associate Justices.
provisions.exact_change: Replace the Supreme Court composition with 1 Chief Justice and 4 Associate Justices.
provisions.evidence: The available number of seats on the United States Supreme Court shall be one (1) Chief Justice and four (4) Associate Justices
provisions.notes: This is a real structural amendment, not a note.

provisions.text_summary: Permanently closes the Court of Appeals.
provisions.exact_change: Mark the Court of Appeals as permanently closed.
provisions.evidence: shall be permanently closed
provisions.notes: Requires textual treatment, not a note dump.

provisions.text_summary: Makes the District of Columbia district court the only federal district court and sets its seat count.
provisions.exact_change: Set the D.C. district court to 1 Chief Judge and 4 Judges and make it the only federal district court.
provisions.evidence: shall be the only federal District Court
provisions.notes: The XML needs real text, not a note.

provisions.text_summary: Limits magistrates to three.
provisions.exact_change: Set magistrates serving under the D.C. district court to three.
provisions.evidence: The available number of Magistrates ... shall be three (3)
provisions.notes: Part of the same structural rewrite.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals and supersedes prior acts affecting the court structure.
provisions.exact_change: Mark the Court of Appeals Temporary Suspension Act of 2021 and conflicting U.S. Codes Act provisions as repealed or superseded.
provisions.evidence: hereby superseded and repealed in full
provisions.notes: This is express repeal language and must be textually recorded.

current_implementation.detail: The current branch uses boilerplate note treatment where the source requires actual textual amendments to Title 28.

recommended_actions: Replace the note with actual Title 28 text amendments.
Carry forward the repeal/supersession clause as real statutory text.
Remove any claim that the operative source was unavailable.

laws.title: Public Law 3-25 | CRACK Act
laws.status_basis: The source text is a large, operative multi-title reform bill with direct amendments to Title 18, Title 5, and Title 50, plus a new administrative framework. The reviewed later enactments do not repeal the entire act.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals the prior National Security Act framework and creates National Security Council rules, threat designations, access rules, and communications limits.
provisions.exact_change: Replace the national-security framework with the new National Security Council and threat-designation structure described in the act.
provisions.evidence: The National Security Act of 2021 shall be repealed in its entirety
provisions.notes: This section is substantive and permanent.

provisions.text_summary: Amends Title 18 definitions for terrorist activities and criminal investigation.
provisions.exact_change: Insert definitions for 'terrorist activities' and 'criminal investigation' and redesignate later paragraphs.
provisions.evidence: Section2510oftitle18,UnitedStatesCode,isamended
provisions.notes: This must be textual, not note-only.

provisions.text_summary: Adds a new Title 18 section authorizing administrative subpoenas in terrorism investigations.
provisions.exact_change: Insert new 18 U.S.C. section 2332j as described in the act.
provisions.evidence: Chapter113Boftitle18,UnitedStatesCode,isamendedbyinserting after section2332i
provisions.notes: The current note treatment is incomplete.

provisions.text_summary: Amends the nondisclosure penalty provision in Title 18.
provisions.exact_change: Replace section 1510(e) with the new penalty language.
provisions.evidence: Section1510(e)oftitle18,UnitedStatesCode,isamendedbystriking the existing provision and replacing with
provisions.notes: A full text amendment is required.

provisions.text_summary: Adds a new Title 18 enforcement section for information requests.
provisions.exact_change: Insert new 18 U.S.C. section 2332k as described in the act.
provisions.evidence: Chapter113Boftitle18,UnitedStatesCode,isamendedbyinserting before section2333
provisions.notes: This is an actual code insertion.

provisions.text_summary: Creates a Title 50 classification scheme for national-security information.
provisions.exact_change: Establish classification standards, levels, authority, and categories in the national-security title.
provisions.evidence: SECTION201.DEFINITIONS ... SECTION205.CLASSIFICATIONCATEGORIES
provisions.notes: This is a permanent framework, not a note.

provisions.text_summary: Creates remedial rules and disclosure protections for law-enforcement surveillance information.
provisions.exact_change: Add the new remedial and disclosure rules described in Title III.
provisions.evidence: SECTION302.APPROPRIATE REMEDIES ... SECTION303.DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION
provisions.notes: The act supplies operative text that should not remain as a note.

provisions.text_summary: Refines Title 18 money-laundering/terrorism-financing language.
provisions.exact_change: Revise section 2339C(c)(2) and 2339C(e) as specified.
provisions.evidence: SECTION420.CORRECTIONSOFFINANCINGOFTERRORISMSTATUTE
provisions.notes: The manifest's numbered section 420 is part of the direct Title 18 work.

provisions.text_summary: Creates criminal intelligence rules and a new racketeering chapter with penalties, civil remedies, venue, evidence, and CID provisions.
provisions.exact_change: Add the criminal-intelligence framework and replace Chapter 96 with the new racketeering chapter.
provisions.evidence: ThereisaCriminalIntelligenceCoordinationCouncil
provisions.notes: The text is substantive and permanent.

current_implementation.detail: The current branch leaves the act as note-only boilerplate even though the source text contains multiple real title amendments and new sections.

recommended_actions: Replace the note dump with actual textual amendments across Titles 18 and 50.
Create the required new sections for the inserted provisions.
Preserve the repeal of the prior National Security Act framework in text, not in a note.

provisions.text_summary: Sets out the private-security and self-defense policy provisions.
provisions.exact_change: Do not keep this as current Code text because the act has been repealed in entirety.
provisions.evidence: Protecting the operations of Private Security and the right to an individual's security
provisions.notes: This is substantive, but it is fully repealed.

laws.title: Public Law 4-29 | Department of Commerce and Labor Act of 2022

provisions.text_summary: Transfers and consolidates the Department of Labor, Department of Commerce, and other agencies into Commerce and Labor.

laws.title: Public Law 4-30 | Fugitive Justice Reform Act
laws.status_basis: The source text is an operative fugitive-list and warrant-database regime with no full repeal found in the reviewed later enactments. It is not merely ceremonial or temporary.

provisions.exact_change: These sections are supporting material for the substantive regime.

provisions.evidence: The Marshal Services shall maintain the list of persons/parties who are ordered onto The Fugitive List

provisions.text_summary: Lists the mechanisms for removal from the fugitive list.
provisions.exact_change: Codify the pardon, sentence-completion, vacatur, and acquittal removal rules.
provisions.evidence: There shall be four mechanisms for removal from The Fugitive List

provisions.text_summary: Repeals H.R. 4 and its amendments.

provisions.evidence: H. R. 4 in its entirety ... shall be repealed and rescinded

recommended_actions: Convert the fugitive-list regime into actual text in Titles 18 and 28.

Remove any claim that the source is merely note-only.
